description: A crisp pastry filled with a savory mixture, commonly spiced potatoes and peas, and then deep-fried or baked. It is widely eaten across South Asia and in many regions influenced by South Asian cuisine.
preparation_steps: Cook the filling and let it cool.
Shape the pastry into cones or triangles and fill them.
Fry or bake until golden and crisp.

health_advice.benefits: Can provide a flavorful snack and may include legumes or vegetables.
health_advice.risks: Deep-fried versions can be high in fat and calories, and sodium can be elevated depending on the filling.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Choose baked versions when available and pair with a fresh chutney or salad rather than heavy sides.

dietary_notes.note: Potato-and-pea samosas are often vegetarian and vegan. Traditional pastry usually contains wheat, so gluten-free versions require alternative dough or wrappers.

allergens: Possible dairy in dough or accompanying sauces
